items.label: peer reviewed journal article
items.description: Article published in a scholarly journal and subject to formal peer review.

items.label: conference paper
items.description: Written paper published in conference proceedings, whether peer reviewed or editorially reviewed.

items.label: conference presentation
items.description: Conference talk, poster, keynote, webinar, panel, or presentation without a corresponding published paper.

items.label: book
items.description: Scholarly or professional monograph, edited volume, or published book-length work.

items.label: book chapter
items.description: Chapter published within an edited volume or handbook.

items.label: dissertation or thesis
items.description: Doctoral dissertation, master's thesis, or other graduate research project.

items.label: government or foundation report
items.description: Report produced by government agencies, foundations, nonprofits, research institutes, or advocacy organizations.

items.label: professional publication
items.description: Trade publication, professional magazine, association newsletter, practitioner journal, or other professionally oriented publication not subject to scholarly peer review.

items.label: toolkit or guidance document
items.description: Handbook, toolkit, implementation guide, curriculum, standards document, policy guide, or training resource intended for practical use.

items.label: blog or web article
items.description: Blog post, website article, online essay, or other informal web publication.

items.label: interview or oral history
items.description: Published interview, oral history, transcript, or recorded conversation used as a source.

items.label: dataset or archive
items.description: Dataset, repository, archive, catalog, collection inventory, or other primary information resource.

items.label: multimedia resource
items.description: Video, podcast, recorded webinar, livestream, or other audiovisual source.

items.label: unpublished document
items.description: Internal report, white paper, working paper, proposal, memorandum, or other unpublished material.

items.label: peer reviewed
items.description: The source was evaluated by independent subject experts before scholarly publication.

items.label: editorial reviewed
items.description: The source was reviewed by an editor or professional publication staff but did not undergo formal scholarly peer review.

items.label: unknown
items.description: Available information does not establish whether the source underwent peer or editorial review.

items.label: not applicable
items.description: Review status is not meaningful for this source type, such as a dataset, archive, or unpublished internal document.

items.label: empirical study
items.description: Research based on the collection and analysis of original data. Includes surveys, interviews, focus groups, observations, experiments, analytics, ethnographies, and mixed-methods studies.

items.label: implementation case
items.description: Research describing, analyzing, or evaluating a specific program, service, collection, partnership, intervention, or design effort in practice.

items.label: content or artifact analysis
items.description: Research that analyzes documents, media, games, collections, archives, policies, metadata, discourse, or other artifacts rather than collecting data directly from participants. Includes content analysis, archival analysis, discourse analysis, and document studies.

items.label: evidence synthesis
items.description: Research that synthesizes existing scholarship rather than generating new primary data. Includes literature reviews, scoping reviews, systematic reviews, meta-analyses, bibliometric studies, and research mappings.

items.label: instrument or framework development
items.description: Work focused on creating, validating, or proposing measures, assessment tools, coding schemes, taxonomies, metadata frameworks, conceptual models, or methodological approaches.

items.label: practitioner reflection
items.description: Professional reports, handbooks, lessons learned, best-practice recommendations, and reflective accounts based primarily on practitioner experience rather than formal research.

items.label: theoretical or conceptual
items.description: Sources that advance arguments, models, definitions, critiques, historical interpretations, or conceptual frameworks without presenting original empirical evidence.

items.label: survey
items.description: Research that collects data through questionnaires or structured instruments administered to individuals or groups.

items.label: qualitative study
items.description: Research based primarily on interviews, focus groups, observations, ethnography, or other qualitative methods used to understand experiences, perspectives, behaviors, or social contexts.

items.label: mixed methods
items.description: Research that intentionally combines qualitative and quantitative methods within a single study.

items.label: experimental study
items.description: Research that tests the effects of an intervention, condition, or treatment by comparing outcomes across groups or conditions.

items.label: case or design study
items.description: An in-depth examination of a specific program, service, game, intervention, organization, community, or design effort within its real-world context.

items.label: content analysis
items.description: Systematic analysis of texts, media, documents, games, policies, collections, or other artifacts to identify themes, patterns, categories, or meanings.

items.label: historical analysis
items.description: Examination of historical documents, records, events, or developments to understand change, continuity, and context over time.

items.label: action research
items.description: A cyclical process in which researchers and practitioners collaboratively implement, evaluate, and refine practices while generating knowledge about the intervention.

items.label: participatory design
items.description: A design approach that actively involves intended users or stakeholders in the creation, development, and refinement of a product, service, program, or intervention.

items.label: evidence synthesis
items.description: Research that reviews, synthesizes, maps, or analyzes existing scholarship. Includes literature reviews, systematic reviews, scoping reviews, meta-analyses, and bibliometric studies.

items.label: theoretical or conceptual
items.description: Research that develops, critiques, compares, or applies theories, models, frameworks, definitions, conceptual arguments, or research agendas without collecting original empirical data.

items.label: none
items.description: No identifiable research method was used because the source is descriptive, opinion-based, informational, or otherwise non-research oriented.

items.label: unspecified
items.description: The source appears to involve research or analysis, but the primary method cannot be determined from the available information.

items.label: academic library
items.description: A college, university, or other postsecondary library serving students, faculty, researchers, and academic programs.

items.label: cultural heritage institution
items.description: A museum, archive, historical society, special collection, preservation organization, memory institution, or related cultural heritage setting where games are discussed as objects of collection, exhibition, preservation, interpretation, or access.

items.label: library school
items.description: A library and information science (LIS) program, school, department, or professional training context focused on preparing current or future library workers.

items.label: multiple library types
items.description: The source explicitly addresses, compares, or synthesizes findings across two or more library types.

items.label: non library context
items.description: A setting outside libraries and related cultural heritage institutions, such as classrooms, community organizations, healthcare settings, commercial venues, homes, or online communities.

items.label: public library
items.description: A publicly funded library serving a general community population through open access to collections, services, programs, and spaces.

items.label: school library
items.description: A library or media center serving students, educators, and curriculum needs within a K–12 educational institution.

items.label: unspecified library context
items.description: The source discusses libraries in general, does not clearly identify a library type, or lacks enough information to determine the setting.

items.label: tabletop game
items.description: Board games, card games, dice games, miniature games, and other rule-based games played primarily on a table or shared physical surface.

items.label: tabletop roleplaying game
items.description: A roleplaying game in which participants use rules, characters, narration, dice, and shared imagination to create a collaborative story.

items.label: live action roleplaying game
items.description: A roleplaying game in which participants physically act out characters and scenarios in real-world space.

items.label: digital game
items.description: A digital game played on a computer, console, mobile device, web browser, or other screen-based platform.

items.label: virtual or augmented reality
items.description: A game or play experience using immersive virtual reality or augmented reality technology.

items.label: escape room or breakout box
items.description: A puzzle-based experience using clues, locks, challenges, narrative, or time pressure to reach a goal.

items.label: game creation
items.description: Activities focused on designing, modifying, programming, prototyping, or otherwise creating games rather than playing existing ones.

items.label: gamification
items.description: The application of game elements such as points, badges, challenges, or leaderboards to a non-game activity or service.

items.label: pervasive game
items.description: A game that extends beyond a dedicated play space and blends gameplay with everyday environments, social interactions, or real-world activities.

items.label: megagame
items.description: A large-scale game involving many participants, often combining roleplaying, simulation, strategy, negotiation, and facilitation.

items.label: toy or open play
items.description: A play object or activity designed for open-ended exploration, construction, imagination, or manipulation rather than formal gameplay.

items.label: multiple game formats
items.description: The source discusses, compares, or incorporates more than one game format without focusing primarily on a single type.

items.label: unspecified game format
items.description: The source discusses games or play without identifying a specific game format.

items.label: collections and access
items.description: Building, managing, cataloging, circulating, preserving, and providing access to games and related materials.

items.label: discovery and advisory
items.description: Helping patrons find, select, interpret, and connect games with other resources through curation, metadata, displays, and advisory services.

items.label: programming and facilitation
items.description: Organizing, delivering, and facilitating game-based events, clubs, workshops, tournaments, instructional activities, and other structured play experiences.

items.label: learning and literacy
items.description: Using games to support learning, information literacy, media literacy, digital literacy, game literacy, research, orientation, and educational goals.

items.label: community and inclusion
items.description: Using games to foster belonging, social connection, inclusion, equity, youth engagement, and community participation.

items.label: creation and making
items.description: Supporting game design, game creation, modding, makerspaces, creative production, and participatory media-making.

items.label: outreach and partnerships
items.description: Extending game-based services through advocacy, marketing, community outreach, and partnerships with organizations and stakeholders.

items.label: professional capacity
items.description: Building organizational capacity through staff training, professional development, assessment, planning, and needs assessment.

items.label: cultural heritage and research
items.description: Preserving, documenting, exhibiting, studying, and providing research access to games as cultural and historical artifacts.

items.label: space and infrastructure
items.description: Designing, maintaining, and managing the physical, digital, and technological environments that support game-based services.

items.label: children
items.description: Primarily intended for elementary-aged children.

items.label: teens
items.description: Primarily intended for adolescents and secondary-school-aged youth.

items.label: young adults
items.description: Primarily intended for emerging adults, college-aged participants, or young adult audiences.

items.label: adults
items.description: Primarily intended for adult participants.

items.label: older adults
items.description: Primarily intended for older adult or senior populations.

items.label: families
items.description: Intended for intergenerational participation involving children and caregivers.

items.label: students
items.description: Intended for learners in formal educational settings where age is not the defining characteristic.

items.label: educators
items.description: Intended for teachers, faculty, librarians, or other educational professionals.

items.label: general public
items.description: Intended for broad community participation without a specific demographic focus.

items.label: underserved or special population
items.description: Intended for a specifically identified population such as disabled patrons, marginalized communities, incarcerated individuals, hospitalized patients, rural populations, or other targeted groups.

items.label: not applicable
items.description: No program audience is identified, or the source is not describing a specific service, collection, or intervention.

items.label: equitable access
items.description: The service improves access to games, technologies, spaces, collections, expertise, or cultural participation.

items.label: discovery and advisory
items.description: The service helps patrons find, select, interpret, or connect games with other library materials and interests.

items.label: learning and literacy
items.description: The service supports information literacy, media literacy, digital literacy, orientation skills, problem solving, critical thinking, or other learning goals.

items.label: engagement and participation
items.description: The service increases attendance, usage, patron interest, motivation, or active participation in library services.

items.label: belonging and community
items.description: The service supports social connection, collaboration, community building, identity exploration, or a sense of belonging.

items.label: creativity and production
items.description: The service supports making, design, storytelling, game creation, modding, or other forms of creative expression.

items.label: wellness and resilience
items.description: The service supports well-being, respite, confidence, mental-health awareness, resilience, or emotionally supportive participation.

items.label: cultural stewardship and preservation
items.description: The service supports collecting, cataloging, describing, preserving, exhibiting, or providing research access to games as cultural objects.

items.label: institutional capacity
items.description: The service improves staff confidence, training, facilitation, infrastructure, implementation capacity, assessment, or sustainable operations.

items.label: not applicable
items.description: The source does not describe or assess a specific program, service, collection, or intervention outcome.
